title: Indentation and Blocks
summary: Understand why indentation controls structure in Python.

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding Indentation
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In Python, indentation is not just for readability; it defines the structure of the code. For example:

```python
if condition:
    # This block is executed if the condition is true
    print('Condition is true')
else:
    # This block is executed if the condition is false
    print('Condition is false')
```

In this example, the indented lines under the `if` and `else` statements are part of their respective blocks. If you forget to indent, Python will raise an `IndentationError`. 

**Your turn:** Try writing your own `if` statement with an `else` block and see how indentation affects the flow.

sketchBlocks.title: Nested Indentation

quizDraft.title: Identifying Correct Indentation
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following code snippets will run without an IndentationError?
options:
if x > 10:
print('x is greater than 10')

if x > 10:
    print('x is greater than 10')
quizDraft.hint: Look for the snippets that maintain proper indentation levels.

help.concept: Indentation in Python defines the blocks of code that belong to control structures like if statements.
help.hint_1: Check if the print statements are indented correctly under their respective if statements.
help.hint_2: Remember that all lines within the same block must have the same level of indentation.

quizDraft.title: Complete the Indentation
quizDraft.prompt:
Fill in the blank to complete the if statement:

```python
if x > 5:
    _____
```
choices: print('x is less than or equal to 5')

quizDraft.title: Indentation Check
quizDraft.prompt: Write a function that checks if a number is positive, negative, or zero. Use proper indentation.

quizDraft.title: Nested Indentation Function
quizDraft.prompt: Create a function that checks if a number is even or odd and also checks if it is positive or negative.

quizDraft.title: Multiple Conditions
quizDraft.prompt: Write a function that checks if a number is between 1 and 10 (inclusive) and returns a message accordingly.
